lines.english: Lesson 18
lines.chinese: 第18课
lines.raw: Lesson 18 | 第18课

lines.english: Electric currents in modern art
lines.chinese: 现代艺术中的电流
lines.raw: Electric currents in modern art | 现代艺术中的电流

lines.english: How might some of the exhibits have been dangerous?
lines.chinese: 有些展品是怎样有危险的呢？
lines.raw: How might some of the exhibits have been dangerous? | 有些展品是怎样有危险的呢？

lines.english: Modern sculpture rarely surprises us any more.
lines.chinese: 现代雕塑已很少令我们感到惊奇了。
lines.raw: Modern sculpture rarely surprises us any more. | 现代雕塑已很少令我们感到惊奇了。

lines.english: The idea that modern art can only be seen in museums is mistaken.
lines.chinese: 现代艺术只能在博物馆里才能看到这种想法是错误的。
lines.raw: The idea that modern art can only be seen in museums is mistaken. | 现代艺术只能在博物馆里才能看到这种想法是错误的。

lines.english: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture on display in public places.
lines.chinese: 即使是对艺术不感兴趣的人，也不会没有注意到在公共场所展出的现代雕塑作品。
lines.raw: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture on display in public places. | 即使是对艺术不感兴趣的人，也不会没有注意到在公共场所展出的现代雕塑作品。

lines.english: Strange forms stand in gardens, and outside buildings and shops.
lines.chinese: 花园里、大楼和商店门外都矗立着奇形怪状的雕塑。
lines.raw: Strange forms stand in gardens, and outside buildings and shops. | 花园里、大楼和商店门外都矗立着奇形怪状的雕塑。

lines.english: We have got quite used to them.
lines.chinese: 我们对它们已习以为常了。
lines.raw: We have got quite used to them. | 我们对它们已习以为常了。

lines.english: Some so-called 'modern' pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years.
lines.chinese: 有些所谓的“现代”作品已经展出了将近80年了。
lines.raw: Some so-called 'modern' pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years. | 有些所谓的“现代”作品已经展出了将近80年了。

lines.english: In spite of this, some people--including myself--were surprised by a recent exhibition of modern sculpture.
lines.chinese: 尽管如此，最近举办的一次现代雕塑展览还是使一些人——包括我本人在内——大吃一惊。
lines.raw: In spite of this, some people--including myself--were surprised by a recent exhibition of modern sculpture. | 尽管如此，最近举办的一次现代雕塑展览还是使一些人——包括我本人在内——大吃一惊。

lines.english: The first thing I saw when I entered the art gallery was a notice which said: 'Do not touch the exhibits, some of them are dangerous!'
lines.chinese: 我走进美术馆时，首先看到的是一块牌子，上面写着：“展品切勿触摸，有些是危险品！”
lines.raw: The first thing I saw when I entered the art gallery was a notice which said: 'Do not touch the exhibits, some of them are dangerous!' | 我走进美术馆时，首先看到的是一块牌子，上面写着：“展品切勿触摸，有些是危险品！”

lines.english: The objects on display were pieces of moving sculpture.
lines.chinese: 展出的作品都是动态雕塑。
lines.raw: The objects on display were pieces of moving sculpture. | 展出的作品都是动态雕塑。

lines.english: Oddly shaped forms that are suspended from the ceiling and move in response to a gust of wind are quite familiar to everybody.
lines.chinese: 悬挂在天花板上随风摆动的奇形怪状的雕塑，大家是相当熟悉的。
lines.raw: Oddly shaped forms that are suspended from the ceiling and move in response to a gust of wind are quite familiar to everybody. | 悬挂在天花板上随风摆动的奇形怪状的雕塑，大家是相当熟悉的。

lines.english: These objects, however, were different.
lines.chinese: 然而，这些展品却与众不同。
lines.raw: These objects, however, were different. | 然而，这些展品却与众不同。

lines.english: Lined up against the wall, there were long thin wires attached to metal spheres.
lines.chinese: 靠墙摆放着的是些系有金属球的长长的细电线。
lines.raw: Lined up against the wall, there were long thin wires attached to metal spheres. | 靠墙摆放着的是些系有金属球的长长的细电线。

lines.english: The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other all the time.
lines.chinese: 金属球都带磁，因此它们总是互相吸引或排斥。
lines.raw: The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other all the time. | 金属球都带磁，因此它们总是互相吸引或排斥。

lines.english: In the centre of the hall,
lines.chinese: 在展览厅中央，
lines.raw: In the centre of the hall, | 在展览厅中央，

lines.english: there were a number of tall structures which contained coloured lights.
lines.chinese: 有些高高的建筑物模型，里面装着彩灯。
lines.raw: there were a number of tall structures which contained coloured lights. | 有些高高的建筑物模型，里面装着彩灯。

lines.english: These lights flickered continuously like traffic lights which have gone mad.
lines.chinese: 这些彩灯不停地闪烁，就像发了疯的红绿灯。
lines.raw: These lights flickered continuously like traffic lights which have gone mad. | 这些彩灯不停地闪烁，就像发了疯的红绿灯。

lines.english: Sparks were emitted from small black boxes and red lamps flashed on and off angrily.
lines.chinese: 小黑盒子里迸出火花，红色灯泡恼怒地闪着。
lines.raw: Sparks were emitted from small black boxes and red lamps flashed on and off angrily. | 小黑盒子里迸出火花，红色灯泡恼怒地闪着。

lines.english: It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment.
lines.chinese: 这简直是史前电子设备展览。
lines.raw: It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment. | 这简直是史前电子设备展览。

lines.english: These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well!
lines.chinese: 这些奇特的造型，似乎不仅要给人以情感上的冲击，还要给人以电击！
lines.raw: These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well! | 这些奇特的造型，似乎不仅要给人以情感上的冲击，还要给人以电击！
